purpose: Validate public source provenance before local extracted PDFs or DART viewer filings are promoted into source-backed narrative claims. Exact document URLs are preferred, but an official source page plus document title, period, local checksum, and access metadata is acceptable when issuers use session-based or dynamic downloads.

rules.rule: Local PDF narrative claims must not be runtime-promoted until public source provenance is attached. Acceptable provenance is a document-level public URL, a stable DART/public filing URL, or an official source page combined with document title, period, local checksum, extraction hash, and evidence locator.

IR index pages are acceptable source-page provenance when an issuer does not expose stable document-level URLs. The claim must still identify the exact local document, covered period, checksum, extraction hash, and evidence locator.
DART viewer filings may use the receipt-number viewer URL as the public document URL, but claim promotion still requires extracted filing text and evidence locators.
URL intake is a paper and runtime gate; it should be validated before any narrative claim manifest is generated.
